checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. All text in the JSON has a direct basis in the source PDF.

The output contains two mailing addresses: '1100 Navaho Drive, Suite 242, Raleigh, NC 27609' and '125 Edinburgh South Drive, Suite 100, Cary, NC 27511.' Both addresses are present in the source document exactly as converted, with the correct formatting (strikethrough and underline) representing the changes. No hallucinated contact info was found.
The source document consists of a single page containing an amendment description, the rule text, and a history note. All these elements are present in the converted JSON.
The source document does not contain any tables; thus, this check is not applicable.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion accurately preserves the original wording. Formatting attributes like bold, italic, underline, and strikethrough are used correctly to represent the legal amendment's intent.

checks.a_analysis: Each text block from the source document (amendment statement, rule title, body paragraph, and history notes) appears exactly once in the output JSON. There is no repetition of sections or paragraphs.

The document contains a single heading '21 NCAC 61 .0102 BOARD OFFICE'. In the output JSON, this is correctly identified as a heading and assigned level 2. Given the document title in the metadata, starting the internal hierarchy at level 2 is standard and logical for accessibility. There are no skipped heading levels.
The source document does not contain any tables, and the output correctly reflects this by containing no table elements.

The output JSON follows the top-to-bottom reading order of the source document perfectly: amendment statement, rule title/heading, the modified paragraph (capturing both original and amended text through formatting flags), and finally the history notes.

The History Note section in the source is a series of lines indented beneath a heading. The output renders these as separate paragraphs. While they could be interpreted as a list, the source does not use formal list markers (bullets or numbers). Converting them to distinct paragraphs preserves the meaning and logical separation of the entries.
The source document contains no hyperlinks. The output JSON correctly identifies all content as plain text (with formatting) and does not inject any fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is faithfully maintained. The HTML output correctly identifies and renders the rule title as a heading, followed by the introductory statement and the rule text. The strike-through and underline formatting used for showing changes to the rule are preserved visually. While the order of the first line and the rule title are swapped to prioritize the heading, this is a standard semantic improvement for web documents and does not confuse the meaning or visual flow.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, there are no tables to evaluate in the output.
The source PDF does not contain any images or logos; therefore, no image elements are required in the output.

There is no evidence of body content duplication in the HTML output. Each segment of text (introductory sentence, rule title, address block, and history note) appears exactly once in the rendered content.
